data.title: DeepSeek Founder's AI War Chest Swells on 57% Gain at Hedge Fund
data.description:
DeepSeek, the Chinese artifical intelligence startup that has emerged as a key global rival to American startup heavyweights OpenAI and Anthropic, may have just gained a new advantage.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

Data from consultation firm Shenzhen PaiPaiWang Investment & Management Co. shows that DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfend's quantative hedge fund, Zhejiang High-Flyer Asset Management, saw an average return of 56.6% acrosss its funds last year. The boost indicates that DeepSeek is riding the boon in China's quantitative hedge funds industry and could help further boost firepower for the Chinese AI startup that is challenging American rivals in terms of the cost of developing artificial intelligent products.

data.title: Archer Aviation (ACHR) CEO Earns Stock Award after Hitting Performance Target
data.description:
A recent Form 4 filing from Archer Aviation (ACHR) might appear to be a cautionary signal from afar, but the details point to steady progress rather than insider selling or near-term dilution. The filing came from Archer's co-founder and Chief Executive Officer, Adam Goldstein, and reflects earned compensation tied to company goals.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This filing shows no stock sale, no cash payout, and no shares entering the market today. Instead, it marks the completion of a defined performance target set more than a year ago.

Meanwhile, ACHR shares rose 1.15% on Friday, closing at $8.81. However, the stock has already shed 1.14% in pre-market trading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Goldman Sachs Pushes Fed Rate Cuts Into 2026 as Inflation Eases
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) now expects the U.S. Federal Reserve to begin cutting interest rates later than previously forecast. The bank now sees two rate cuts of 0.25% each in June and September 2026, instead of earlier moves in March and June. As a result, interest rates are expected to remain higher for longer than markets once anticipated. Goldman now expects the Fed funds rate to end 2026 in a range of 3% to 3.25%, reflecting a slower and more cautious policy shift.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Goldman lowered its 12-month recession risk estimate to 20% from 30%. This change indicates continued economic stability rather than growing stress. Recent data show that U.S. growth has been stronger than expected, even as parts of the economy begin to cool. As a result, the Fed has more room to wait before easing policy.

Why Goldman Changed Its View

The update follows softer U.S. jobs data, which points to a labor market that is slowly losing momentum. However, hiring conditions remain stable and do not signal a sudden downturn. At the same time, inflation continues to move closer to the Fed target, reducing pressure for early rate cuts. Goldman also noted that progress made during 2025 was partly hidden by temporary tariff effects that are now fading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Heineken CEO Dolf van den Brink Resigns as Beer Sales and Shares Falter
data.description:
Netherlands-based Heineken's (HEINY) CEO Dolf van den Brink is stepping down after nearly six years as the top boss at the company. The brewer said he will leave on May 31 and that the search for his successor has begun. The resignation comes as Heineken faces declining beer sales and a challenging global market.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Overall, van den Brink has spent over 28 years at Heineken, including six as the CEO, and made the decision to exit in consultation with the Supervisory Board. According to the company, he will continue to support the company in an advisory role for eight months starting June 1.

RBC Weighs in on CEO Exit

RBC's four-star-rated analyst James Edwardes Jones described Heineken's performance under van den Brink as underwhelming. Jones stated that the brewer has delivered a negative shareholder return of 9% since his appointment on June 1, 2020.

According to Jones, this significantly lagged competitors such as Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), which returned 36%, and Carlsberg (CABGY), which gained 12%. Heineken also underperformed the MSCI European consumer staples sector, which rose 22% over the same period.

Jones has a Hold rating on Heineken's Netherlands-listed shares (NL:HEIA) with a €76 price target. Earlier this month, he downgraded the stock from Buy to Hold.

Heineken Struggles as Global Beer Market Slows

Heineken is facing mounting challenges as the global beer market slows. Falling sales in key regions like Europe and the U.S., combined with shifting consumer drinking habits, have weighed on the brewer's growth and profits.

In October, Heineken warned that annual profits would come in below expectations, citing weaker growth. The company said it now expects adjusted operating profit growth for the fiscal year to hit the lower end of its previously forecast range of 4% to 8%. It also revised its volume outlook, predicting a modest decline instead of the stable volumes it had anticipated earlier.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/12/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall on Trump-Powell Tensions, Big Bank Earnings Ahead
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down early Monday as investors reacted to President Donald Trump's subpoena of Federal Chair Jerome Powell. Markets are also getting ready for the Q4 earnings season, which begins this week with major banks reporting first.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.76%, 0.52%, and 0.46%, respectively, at 3:47 a.m. EST on January 12.

During Friday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed higher, with the S&P 500 and the 30-stock Dow closing at record highs. Despite a volatile trading week, the indexes ended in positive territory. The S&P 500 climbed over 1%, the Dow advanced 2.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite increased 1.9%.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch key economic reports, including the important Consumer Price Index (CPI) data due on Tuesday, Producer Price Index (PPI) data, retail sales, and weekly initial jobless claims.

Moreover, the fourth-quarter earnings season is set to open, with major banks reporting this week. Companies reporting include JPMorgan (JPM), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Morgan Stanley (MS).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.19%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.07 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,583 per ounce on Monday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mostly lower on Monday amid heightened geopolitical tensions related to Iran and Greenland.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded higher on January 12, after U.S. President Donald Trump's comments to intervene in Iran's ongoing unrest.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index surged 1.44%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 1.09%, while the Shenzhen Component added 2.40%. Meanwhile, Japan's markets remained closed for a public holiday.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Apple (AAPL) Leads Smartphone Market in 2025 Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
This article was written by Shalu Saraf and reviewed by Gilan Miller-Gertz.

Apple (AAPL) was the world's top smartphone maker in 2025, holding a 20% global market share, according to new data from Counterpoint Research. Apple led all brands on strong demand from emerging and mid-sized markets. Sales of the iPhone 17 series also helped drive shipments, giving Apple the largest share among the top five smartphone makers, Counterpoint said.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The update comes ahead of Apple's Q1 Fiscal 2026 earnings, due on January 29. Wall Street expects Apple to report earnings of $2.67 per share, up about 11% year-over-year. Revenue is forecasted to rise 10.5% from last year to $137.41 billion, based on TipRanks data.

Global Shipments Rose Modestly

Global smartphone shipments rose 2% year over year in 2025, supported by improved demand and better economic conditions in emerging markets. The gain marks a steady recovery after several weak years for the industry.

After Apple, Samsung (SSNLF) ranked second with a 19% share, showing modest shipment growth. Xiaomi (XIACF) placed third with a 13% share, helped by steady demand in emerging markets.

Early in 2025, many manufacturers shipped devices sooner than planned to get ahead of possible tariffs. That effect faded later in the year, leaving second-half shipment volumes largely unchanged.

Outlook Turns Cautious for 2026

Looking ahead, Counterpoint expects the global smartphone market to soften in 2026. Chip shortages and rising component costs are likely to weigh on production.

At the same time, chipmakers are shifting capacity toward AI data centers, which could limit supply for smartphones. While demand remains stable in some regions, higher costs and tighter supply may slow growth.

For Apple, the strong 2025 result shows its ability to gain share even in a slow market. Investors will now watch how supply limits and higher costs affect smartphone demand in 2026.

Is Apple a Buy or Sell Right Now?  

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on AAPL stock based on 19 Buys, 11 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AAPL price target of $299.49 per share implies 15.47% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: XPeng (XPEV) Hires Banks for Hong Kong IPO of Aridge Flying Car Unit
data.description:
Chinese electric vehicle (EV) maker XPeng (XPEV) has hired JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Morgan Stanley (MS) to work on an initial public offering (IPO) of its flying car unit Aridge in Hong Kong, Bloomberg reported. According to sources, the company has filed confidentially for a public listing in Hong Kong, which may take place as soon as this year. Talks are underway, and the IPO plan could change, cautioned sources.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

XPeng Has Big Plans for Aridge Flying Car Unit

XPeng's flying car unit was originally named XPeng Aeroht and was renamed Aridge in October 2025. The company said that the new name reflects its vision to be the dominant player in low-altitude flight product innovation. The unit has a flying car factory in Guangzhou, China, that supports modern assembly line production. Deliveries are targeted to commence this year. Reportedly, banks were invited last year to pitch for roles on a potential XPEng Aeroht IPO in Hong Kong or the U.S.

Interestingly, Aridge received $250 million in Series B financing in July 2025, marking its most recent funding round. At the AI Day in November 2025, XPeng unveiled the A868, Aridge's new offering with a hybrid powertrain and a range exceeding 500 kilometers. The A868 targets multi-passenger long-distance travel.

Is XPEV Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on XPeng stock based on four Buys, one Hold, and one Sell recommendation. The average XPEV stock price target of $29.67 indicates 48.2% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: FB, Insta, Threads: Meta Deletes 550,000 Accounts Due to Australia's Under-16 Ban
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META), the parent of Facebook, Instagram, and Threads, deleted about 550,000 social media accounts in Australia to comply with the under-16 age ban. Australia's world-first social media ban for children took effect on December 10, 2025, and Meta had already started sending notices to suspected under-16 account holders about the closures.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta revealed these exact numbers in a statement today, becoming the first company to do so. The social media giant removed 330,639 Instagram accounts, 173,497 Facebook accounts, and 39,916 Threads accounts between December 4 and December 11, 2025. The 10 platforms subject to this ban include Snapchat (SNAP), ByteDance's TikTok, Google's (GOOGL) YouTube, Reddit (RDDT), X, and Amazon's (AMZN) Twitch.

Why This Ban Matters

Australia's Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act 2025 restricts children aged 13 to 15 from creating or maintaining accounts, due to fears of negative influences from these platforms. Social media companies are required to use age verification methods beyond self-reporting to remove such accounts, or face fines of up to A$49.5 million ($33 million).

Meta uses UK-based Yoti for verifying the age of account holders. Users can submit video selfies or government IDs to confirm their age, with Yoti reportedly holding all data securely. Despite Meta's global 13+ age minimum, some children under 13 used fake birth dates or parental help to create accounts.

Although Meta vowed to comply with the law, it argued that the legislation lacks an industry-wide age standard. Meta suggested verifying age at app installation and requiring parental consent for under-16 users as a more effective approach. Instead of an outright ban, the company advocates strengthening privacy and age-appropriate online experiences, while urging the Australian government to collaborate constructively with industry stakeholders. The Australian government plans to release data later this week on the number of young Australians removed from affected platforms.

Is META Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On TipRanks, META stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 37 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Meta Platforms price target of $824.90 implies 26.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, META shares have gained 7.4%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Tempus AI (TEM) Tops $1.1B in Total Contract Value as Pharma Demand for AI Tools Surges
data.description:
Tempus AI's (TEM) preliminary results show its total contract value (TCV) surpassed $1.1 billion, marking the highest level in its history. The update reflects how quickly the AI‑driven healthcare company is growing as drugmakers increasingly rely on its multimodal data and AI-driven tools.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company estimates that its Data and Applications segment revenue reached about $316 million for the full‑year 2025, reflecting about 31% year‑over‑year growth. Its Insights unit, which includes data licensing, grew even faster at 38%.

Also, the Diagnostics unit revenue is expected at about $955 million, up about 111% from last year. The upside will likely be driven by a 26% increase in oncology testing and a 29% increase in hereditary testing.

The company plans to release full fourth‑quarter and full‑year 2025 results during its earnings call in February 2026.

Big Pharma Demand Is Fueling Momentum 

Tempus said it signed over 70 data agreements in 2025, spanning major pharmaceutical companies such as AstraZeneca (AZN), GSK (GSK), Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY), Pfizer (PFE), Novartis (NVS), Merck (MRK), AbbVie (ABBV), Eli Lilly (LLY), and more.

The company's multimodal dataset, which combines genomic, clinical, imaging, and other data types, is being used more often in drug discovery and development. This surge in demand is a key driver behind the company's record TCV.

Looking ahead, CEO Eric Lefkofsky said, "We enter 2026 in an exceptionally strong position with both of our main businesses accelerating in growth and delivering the financial leverage inherent in our platform. With AI as a catalyst across all of our products, we couldn't be more excited for 2026."

Is Tempus AI a Good Stock?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Tempus AI stock based on five Buy and six Hold recommendations. The average TEM stock price target of $86 indicates 29.77% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Why Chinese AI Stocks Are Rallying Today—Is This the Next Big Tech Bull Run?
data.description:
Chinese AI stocks surged sharply today, extending a rally that began last week as investor excitement around new IPOs, chip innovations, and strong earnings forecasts lifted sentiment across the sector. Investors are now wondering whether this momentum is just a short-term boost or the start of a broader tech bull run, with China's largest AI and chipmakers showing signs of accelerating growth and strong market demand.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What's Happening with Chinese Tech Stocks?

On Monday, China's AI startups MiniMax (HK:0100) and Zhipu AI topped the sector after strong IPO performances. Zhipu, which trades as Knowledge Atlas Tech Joint Stock (HK:2513), surged over 40%, while competitor MiniMax rose over 25% in Hong Kong. At the same time, chipmaker Shanghai Iluvatar CoreX SemiCon (HK:9903) gained nearly 9% after a solid debut on the market.

Among the big Chinese names, Baidu (BIDU) rose 4% in Hong Kong, extending last week's rally after reports said it filed to list its chip unit, Kunlunxin. Tencent (TCEHY) gained 2.1%, while Alibaba (BABA) jumped over 5% on news that Beijing may step in to ease an ongoing price war in the sector.

Elsewhere in Asia, TSMC's (TSM) Taiwan shares rose 1.4% after the world's largest contract chipmaker reported strong year-on-year growth in December sales. The upbeat results, along with Nvidia's (NVDA) new chip launch and positive comments at last week's CES trade show, helped lift sentiment across AI stocks.

Is China Tech the Next Big Winner?

Overall, the Chinese AI sector is still recovering from heavy losses in late 2025, when concerns over high valuations and overcrowded AI trades weighed on investor confidence.

Looking ahead to 2026, China's tech sector is showing early signs of a comeback, led by a rebound in AI stocks. Government support and easing regulatory pressure are also boosting investor confidence. At the same time, Chinese tech and AI stocks are attracting attention from investors seeking alternatives to expensive U.S. shares, as they offer lower valuations with similar growth potential.

In December, UBS called Chinese tech stocks the "most attractive," citing rapid AI monetization and Beijing's push for technological self-reliance.

Having said that, risks remain, including policy uncertainty and uneven growth, so investors are favoring AI, chips, and leading platforms rather than the entire sector.
